top_videos.title: Clay, Pomade, Gel or Cream? | Men's Hair Product Guide

top_objections.text: This video wouldn't happen to be sponsored by bumble and bumble right? 🤣🤣
2:56 a product is NOT cruelty free if it has lanolin. Its a labeling scam
Depends how long "quite long" is. Clays are good for I'd say 6 inches or less, but anything more than that they won't help.

top_positive_signals.text: Which one would you recommend for a completely bald man? They all just make my head look shiny.
Damn that hairstyle using the cream really suits you. I would love it if you make a tutorial for styling that hairstyle
Love how gels give that defined look, though not my go-to. I balance my styling experiments with nordic biolabbs in my routine.

great breakdown, no matter what you use though some level of chemical damage is kind of inevitable. that’s why i started balancing it out with an all-natural shampoo and red light therapy (i’m using the nordic biolabs laser cap, 15 minutes, 3 x a week). it’s made a big difference for keeping my hair healthy underneath all the styling.
Such a great idea! And you matched the products to your backgrounds! I was upset when it ended! You continue to inspire me and my channel! 👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻
I love matte clay, it is super easy to use and it looks very clean and natural without the gloss.
Towel dry, apply hair product, and then comb it and hair dry it into preferred style, and you shd see results! You can always touch it up during the day with a comb or with your hands
Hi Robin I just want to say thank you, you have helped me greatly in the hair department I really do trust what you say, your honesty is refreshing. Your amazing

I've got thin long hair that I wear pulled back. I don't like of using heavy hair gels, and this littlllextra neatfix hair finishing stick makes for a great alternative. It holds all of the stray hairs in place without feeling heaviness. Plus, it’s really easy to use thanks to the wand/brush. Additionally, it held my hair in place for the majority of the day!

As far as your hair goes based on this video I'd say creams should be your go to. The loose "flowy" styles work well with you. 
As for me, I'm a paste and sometimes clay kind of guy for casual days. Lately though I've been loving Crystal Lake from Shear Revival.
